purpose: Provide a real-time monitoring threshold for switching between carry-favorable and carry-adverse FX regimes.
components: Threshold level: ~9.5% G10 FX realized volatility (Hansen threshold regression, p<0.01)
Below threshold regime: carry +0.58%/month (t=3.87, n=360 months) — positive expected returns
Above threshold regime: carry -1.18%/month (t=-2.11, n=144 months) — negative expected returns
Current reading: G10 FX vol at 7-9%, near the threshold — knife-edge positioning
Vol-of-vol as supplementary monitoring metric, potentially more informative than vol level post-2015
Three-zone classification: below 9% realized G10 FX vol (rate differentials explain 8-12%), 9-12% transitional zone, above 12% (rate differentials explain 0-2%)
Risk management application: monitor proximity, reduce position size as vol approaches threshold
Explicit non-actionability: NOT a trading or hedging signal due to tautological issues and out-of-sample degradation
current_assessment: Currently in the low-vol regime where rate differentials have explanatory power. Compressed spring configuration (extreme divergence + low vol) is active, suggesting transition risk is elevated.
limitations: Partially tautological: carry drawdowns mechanically produce volatility, so conditioning on vol is partly conditioning on the outcome
Threshold has wider confidence intervals post-2015
Out-of-sample improvement from vol timing is near zero according to some analysts
Moreira & Muir (2017) show only modest 0.1-0.2 Sharpe improvement, contested by others
The vol signal typically arrives after most drawdown damage has already occurred
Partly tautological: carry drawdowns ARE high-vol events by definition
Out-of-sample vol-timing Sharpe degrades from 0.2-0.4 to 0.05-0.15 after transaction costs
By the time realized vol reaches threshold, initial carry drawdown has already occurred
Causal direction unresolved without instrumental variable approach
